Code fragment: var rsuTimeData = context.select(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION.SIMTIME).distinctOn(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION.SIMTIME).from(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION).fetchInto(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION);
Explanation: The query selects only SIMTIME but then fetches into the full RSUINFORMATION record, which causes jOOQ to map all columns of the table. Consider using `.fetch()` or `.fetchInto(Double.class)` to only retrieve the SIMTIME column.

Code fragment: var allRSUData = context.select().from(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION).orderBy(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION.SIMTIME).fetch();
Explanation: Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the RSUINFORMATION table. Explicitly list only the required columns (e.g., RSU_ID, X, Y, COMMUNICATION_RADIUS, MAX_VEHICLE_COMMUNICATION, SIMTIME) to improve query performance and clarity.

Code fragment: var diffRSU =  context.select().distinctOn(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION.RSU_ID).from(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION).fetchInto(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION);
Explanation: Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the RSUINFORMATION table. Since only the count of distinct RSU_IDs is needed, consider using `selectCount()` or `select(Rsuinformation.RSUINFORMATION.RSU_ID)` instead.
